title: Campynemanthe neocaledonica
taxonomy: Campynemanthe neocaledonica is a species of flowering plant in the family Campynemataceae. It belongs to the order Asparagales and is native to New Caledonia, an island in the Pacific Ocean. The plant is a member of the genus Campynemanthe, which is a small group of flowering plants. Campynemanthe neocaledonica is known to thrive in humid and subtropical environments. It is a relatively rare and lesser-known species within the plant kingdom.

intro: Campynemanthe neocaledonica, commonly known as New Caledonian Campynemanthe, is a species of flowering plant in the family Campynemataceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Campynemanthe neocaledonica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
